meta.description: Train support vector machine classifiers and assess feature importance via recursive feature elimination (RFE). Inputs: feature matrix, binary class labels. Outputs: trained SVM model, RFE importance ranking, optimal feature subset, ROC performance curve.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated scientific claims or invented performance numbers were observed in any output.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill stayed within a computational feature-ranking workflow and made no diagnostic or prescriptive claims.

methodological_ground.detail: The documented two-class linear SVM-RFE approach matched the tested binary dataset and scope restrictions.

code_usability.detail: Canonical, custom, missing-bundle, plot-only reuse, and corrupt-bundle executions all behaved as documented during the audit.

functional_suitability.note: The skill covers the main binary SVM-RFE workflow, plot-only reuse, scope limits, and reproducibility expectations clearly.

reliability.note: Input validation, path confinement, and recovery guidance are strong; the main remaining gap is limited automated coverage for negative parameter cases.

performance_context.note: Documentation is layered well and the execution path is linear with no redundant steps.

agent_usability.note: Instructions are easy to follow and outputs are well specified; the only deduction is that one selection heuristic is implicit rather than user-controlled.

human_usability.note: Trigger language is natural, runnable examples are present, and out-of-scope boundaries are clearly called out.

security.note: Path confinement, no network activity, and no dynamic code execution were all confirmed.

maintainability.note: Scripts are modular and tests exist, but the automated suite can still cover more documented failure branches.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision, progressive disclosure, composability, idempotency, and escape hatches are all strong.

inputs.label: Bundled binary SVM-RFE run
inputs.note: Canonical run completed successfully and produced the documented bundle, tables, plots, and session info.

assertions.text: Command completes successfully and logs the expected major stages
assertions.note: Input reading, analysis, plotting, and successful completion were all logged.

assertions.text: The documented analysis artifacts are created in the requested output directory
assertions.note: The result bundle, two CSV tables, two PDF plots, and session info file were present.

assertions.text: The selected feature table is consistent with the ranking output
assertions.note: The selected-feature CSV was a prefix of the full ranking table.

assertions.text: The workflow stays within the documented scope
assertions.note: No preprocessing, remote fetching, or dynamic code execution was observed.

inputs.label: Tolerance-rule custom run
inputs.note: Custom tolerance-rule settings executed cleanly and produced a full output set in a separate directory.

assertions.text: Custom feature-selection settings execute successfully
assertions.note: The tolerance rule, tolerance value, and custom plotting options were accepted without errors.

assertions.text: The run still produces the expected result bundle, tables, and plots
assertions.note: All expected artifacts were written under the custom output directory.

assertions.text: The skill remains reproducible under fixed seed settings
assertions.note: The custom run completed deterministically with the requested seed.

assertions.text: The workflow remains scoped to two-class SVM-RFE ranking
assertions.note: No unrelated preprocessing or alternate modeling workflow was triggered.

inputs.label: Missing plot-only bundle
inputs.note: Missing plot-only artifact was rejected with a clear standardized error and an obvious recovery path.

assertions.text: Plot-only mode rejects missing result bundles before plotting
assertions.note: Execution stopped immediately and did not attempt plot generation.

assertions.text: The error uses a standardized skill error code
assertions.note: The error began with SKILL_FILE_NOT_FOUND.

assertions.text: The error is actionable and recoverable
assertions.note: The message states that plot-only mode requires an existing model file.

assertions.text: The failure is safe and scoped
assertions.note: No undefined processing or unsafe side effects were observed.

inputs.label: Successful plot-only regeneration
inputs.note: Existing result bundles were reused cleanly and plots were regenerated without rerunning analysis.

assertions.text: Existing result bundles can be reused without re-reading raw inputs
assertions.note: The logs skipped input-reading and analysis and went straight to plot generation.

assertions.text: Plot regeneration succeeds with the documented minimal arguments
assertions.note: The plot-only command completed successfully with only output directory, seed, and timeout parameters.

assertions.text: The command avoids unnecessary reruns of the full SVM-RFE workflow
assertions.note: Only the plotting phase ran during the plot-only invocation.

assertions.text: Behavior remains deterministic on the same stored bundle
assertions.note: Repeated reuse of the same bundle completed consistently.

inputs.label: Corrupt plot-only bundle
inputs.note: The corrupt RDS bundle was rejected with a standardized skill error and explicit recovery guidance.

assertions.text: Corrupt svm_result.rds input is rejected instead of producing undefined plots
assertions.note: Execution stopped immediately after readRDS failed.

assertions.text: Corrupt bundle errors use a standardized SKILL_* code
assertions.note: The message was wrapped as SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER instead of surfacing only the raw R error.

assertions.text: Corrupt bundle errors provide actionable recovery guidance
assertions.note: The error tells the user to rerun the full analysis to regenerate the saved bundle.

assertions.text: Failure handling remains non-destructive and within scope
assertions.note: No unsafe side effects or out-of-root writes were observed.

key_strengths: The skill has a strong CLI contract with clear inputs, outputs, and scope boundaries for two-class SVM-RFE work.
Path confinement is implemented correctly: output traversal outside the skill root was rejected with a standardized error.
Determinism is strong: repeat canonical runs produced byte-identical ranking CSV outputs under a fixed seed.
The implementation is modular and backed by a runnable packaged test suite that passed during the audit.

recommendations.title: Silence expected CLI warning noise in tests
recommendations.problem: The packaged test suite passes, but intentional negative tests still emit a system2 warning about non-zero exit status, which makes the audit and CI logs noisier than necessary.
recommendations.root_cause: test_skill.R captures expected failures with system2, and R still warns when a subprocess exits with status 1 even when that status is being asserted deliberately.
recommendations.fix: Wrap expected-failure system2 calls in suppressWarnings or a small helper that treats non-zero exit status as data, then assert on the returned status and output explicitly. This keeps the test logs clean without weakening coverage.

recommendations.title: Expand negative-path regression coverage
recommendations.problem: Manual audit checks confirmed output-dir path confinement and argument validation, but these documented branches are not all covered by the packaged test suite.
recommendations.root_cause: Automated tests currently focus on the main happy path plus plot-only bundle scenarios and leave some validation branches to manual verification.
recommendations.fix: Add regression tests for path traversal attempts, invalid numeric parameters, and unsupported selector values so the documented contract stays locked in as the skill evolves.

recommendations.title: Document group-column selection heuristic
recommendations.problem: The implementation auto-detects the group column by scanning for the case and control labels or a column name containing 'group', but this fallback behavior is not described in the public contract.
recommendations.root_cause: detect_group_column() contains a helpful heuristic that remains implicit in the documentation.
recommendations.fix: Document the current heuristic in SKILL.md and cli-guide.md, or add an explicit --group_column argument if deterministic column selection needs to be user-controlled.
